root_text.an3.128:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bārāṇasiyaṁ viharati isipatane migadāye.
root_text.an3.128:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya bārāṇasiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.an3.128:1.3: Addasā kho bhagavā goyogapilakkhasmiṁ piṇḍāya caramāno aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ rittassādaṁ bāhirassādaṁ muṭṭhassatiṁ asampajānaṁ asamāhitaṁ vibbhantacittaṁ pākatindriyaṁ.
root_text.an3.128:2.2: Taṁ vata, bhikkhu, kaṭuviyakataṁ attānaṁ āmagandhena avassutaṁ makkhikā nānupatissanti nānvāssavissantīti, netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjatī”ti.
root_text.an3.128:2.3: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhagavatā iminā ovādena ovadito saṁvegamāpādi.
root_text.an3.128:2.4: Atha kho bhagavā bārāṇasiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an3.128:3.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhikkhave, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya bārāṇasiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ.
root_text.an3.128:3.2: Addasaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, goyogapilakkhasmiṁ piṇḍāya caramāno aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ rittassādaṁ bāhirassādaṁ muṭṭhassatiṁ asampajānaṁ asamāhitaṁ vibbhantacittaṁ pākatindriyaṁ.
root_text.an3.128:4.2: Taṁ vata bhikkhu kaṭuviyakataṁ attānaṁ āmagandhena avassutaṁ makkhikā nānupatissanti nānvāssavissantīti, netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjatī’ti.
root_text.an3.128:4.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, so bhikkhu mayā iminā ovādena ovadito saṁvegamāpādī”ti.
root_text.an3.128:4.4: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.128:5.4: Taṁ vata, bhikkhu, kaṭuviyakataṁ attānaṁ āmagandhena avassutaṁ makkhikā nānupatissanti nānvāssavissantīti, netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjatīti.

translation_text.an3.128:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana.
translation_text.an3.128:1.2: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Varanasi for alms.
translation_text.an3.128:1.3: While the Buddha was walking for alms near the cow-hitching place at the wavy leaf fig, he saw a disgruntled monk who was looking for pleasure in external things, unmindful, without situational awareness or immersion, with straying mind and undisciplined faculties.
translation_text.an3.128:2.2: If you’re bitter, festering with putrefaction, flies will, without a doubt, plague and infest you.”
translation_text.an3.128:2.3: Hearing this advice of the Buddha, that monk was struck with a sense of urgency.
translation_text.an3.128:2.4: Then, after the meal, on his return from almsround, the Buddha told the mendicants what had happened. …
translation_text.an3.128:4.4: When he said this, one of the mendicants asked the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.128:5.3: and bad, unskillful thoughts are the flies.
translation_text.an3.128:5.4: If you’re bitter, festering with putrefaction, flies will, without a doubt, plague and infest you.
translation_text.an3.128:6.1: When your eyes and ears are unguarded,
translation_text.an3.128:6.2: and you’re not restrained in your sense faculties,
translation_text.an3.128:8.2: if they don’t find serenity in themselves,
translation_text.an3.128:9.3: they, being peaceful, sleep at ease,
translation_text.an3.128:9.4: since they’ve got rid of the flies.”

variant_text.an3.128:1.3: goyogapilakkhasmiṁ → goyogamilakkhasmiṁ (sya-all, km, mr) | caramāno → caramānaṁ (mr)
variant_text.an3.128:2.1: kho tvaṁ, bhikkhu → bhikkhu bhikkhu mā kho tvaṁ (bj, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.128:2.2: āmagandhena → āmagandhe (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | nānvāssavissantīti → nānvassavissanti (pts1ed); nānubandhissanti (mr)
